---
title: "analysis code"
author: "Grace Manninen"
date: "2025-03-24"
output: html_document
---
loading in packages/libraries
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(purrr)
library(stringr)
library(ggplot2)

library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(emmeans)
library(jsonlite)
library(readr)
library(tools)
```

define favoured/unfavoured images
```{r}
favouredImages <- c(
  "images/mammal17.png","images/mammal31.png","images/mammal23.png","images/mammal16.png",
  "images/mammal19.png","images/mammal13.png","images/mammal14.png","images/mammal25.png",
  "images/fish15.png","images/mammal35.png"
)

unfavouredImages <- c(
  "images/amphibian1.png","images/amphibian3.png","images/amphibian4.png",
  "images/arachnid1.png","images/arachnid3.png","images/bivalve1.png",
  "images/insect2.png","images/insect3.png","images/insect8.png","images/insect13.png"
)
```

Set directory with Raw Data 
```{r}
datadir <- "/Users/gracemanninen/Desktop/Experiment - Thesis/pilot data/"
files <- list.files(datadir, pattern = "\\.txt$", full.names = TRUE)
```

Read and Combine all .txt files
```{r}
raw_data <- map_dfr(files, function(f) {
  read_file(f) %>%
    str_split("\n") %>%
    first() %>%
    discard(~ .x == "") %>%
    map_dfr(fromJSON, flatten = TRUE) %>%
    mutate(
      source_file = basename(f),
      participant_id = file_path_sans_ext(basename(f))
    )
})

# Add species type column
raw_data <- raw_data %>%
  mutate(
    species_type = case_when(
      image %in% favouredImages ~ "favoured",
      image %in% unfavouredImages ~ "unfavoured",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_   # For images that are neither
    )
  )
```


```{r}
# Separate trials
image_trials <- raw_data %>% filter(trial_type == "image-button-response")
audio_trials <- raw_data %>% filter(trial_type == "audio-button-response")
likert_trials <- raw_data %>% filter(trial_type == "survey-likert")

# Quick check
table(image_trials$trial_type)
table(audio_trials$trial_type)
table(likert_trials$trial_type)
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)

# Extract image trials from html-button-response
image_trials <- raw_data %>%
  filter(trial_type == "html-button-response") %>%
  mutate(
    image = str_extract(stimulus, "images/[^\"']+\\.png")
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(image)) %>%   # keep only rows that actually contain images
  select(participant_id, trial_index, image, music, audioFile, species_type)

# Check
head(image_trials, 20)
nrow(image_trials)
```



```{r}
likert_trials <- raw_data %>%
  filter(trial_type == "survey-likert") %>%
  select(participant_id, trial_index, response) %>%
  mutate(
    likert_score = as.numeric(response)
  )

# Check
head(likert_trials, 20)
nrow(likert_trials)
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)

# Add order within each participant
image_trials <- image_trials %>%
  group_by(participant_id) %>%
  mutate(order = row_number()) %>%
  ungroup()

likert_trials <- likert_trials %>%
  group_by(participant_id) %>%
  mutate(order = row_number()) %>%
  ungroup()

# Join them
combined_data <- likert_trials %>%
  left_join(image_trials, by = c("participant_id", "order"))

# Check result
head(combined_data, 20)
nrow(combined_data)
```

```{r}
likert_trials$response[1]
str(likert_trials$response)

likert_trials <- likert_trials %>%
  mutate(
    likert_score = sapply(response, function(x) {
      if (is.list(x)) {
        as.numeric(x[[1]])
      } else {
        as.numeric(x)
      }
    })
  )

head(likert_trials,20)
summary(likert_trials$likert_score)
```

```{r}
# Join datasets
combined_data <- likert_trials %>%
  left_join(image_trials, by = c("participant_id", "order"))

# Keep only what we need
combined_data <- combined_data %>%
  mutate(
    music_condition = ifelse(is.na(audioFile), "no_music", "music")
  ) %>%
  select(participant_id, likert_score, image, species_type, audioFile, music_condition)

# Check
head(combined_data, 20)
nrow(combined_data)
```

```{r}
plot_data <- combined_data %>%
  group_by(music_condition, species_type) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_score = mean(likert_score, na.rm = TRUE),
    se = sd(likert_score, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

plot_data

library(ggplot2)

ggplot(plot_data, aes(
  x = interaction(music_condition, species_type),
  y = mean_score,
  fill = music_condition
)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  geom_errorbar(aes(
    ymin = mean_score - se,
    ymax = mean_score + se
  ), width = 0.2) +
  labs(
    x = "Condition",
    y = "Mean Likert Score",
    title = "Pro-Conservation Attitudes by Music and Species Type"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

SIMULATED DATA 
```{r}
set.seed(123)
n_participants <- 40

datasim <- data.frame()

for (p in 1:n_participants) 
  
  simulate_likert <- function(n, mean, sd) {
  round(pmin(pmax(rnorm(n, mean, sd), 0), 6)) # round and clamp to 1–7
  }
  
  # 5 ratings per condition
  music_fav <- simulate_likert(5, mean = 5.7, sd = 0.2)
  music_unfav <- simulate_likert(5, mean = 4, sd = 0.2)
  nomusic_fav <- simulate_likert(5, mean = 4.6, sd = 0.2)
  nomusic_unfav <- simulate_likert(5, mean = 1, sd = 0.3)
  
n_participants <- 40 
demographics_age <- sample( c('16-19', '20-24', '25-29', '30-34', '35-39', '40-44', '45-49', '50+', 'I choose not to say'), n_participants, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.30, 0.30, 0.20, 0.07, 0.05, 0.03, 0.02, 0.02, 0.01) ) 

demographics_levelofstudy <- sample( c('DID NOT COMPLETE HIGHSCHOOL', 'COMPLETED HIGHSCHOOL', 'CURRENTLY IN UNDERGRAD', 'COMPLETED UNDERGRAD', 'CURRENTLY IN MASTERS', 'COMPLETED MASTERS', 'IN PHD', 'COMPLETED PHD', 'I choose not to say'), n_participants, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.01, 0.04, 0.70, 0.10, 0.05, 0.03, 0.03, 0.03, 0.01) ) 

demographics_area <- sample( c('RURAL', 'URBAN', 'I choose not to say'), n_participants, replace = TRUE ) 

demographics_gender <- sample( c('woman', 'man', 'non-binary person', 'I choose not to say'), n_participants, replace = TRUE )

participantsim <- rep(1:40, each = 20) 
musicsim <- rep(c(rep("yes", 10), rep("no", 10)), 40)
species_typesim <- rep(c(rep("favoured", 5), rep("unfavoured", 5), rep("favoured", 5), rep("unfavoured", 5)), 40) 

ratingsim <- c(music_favsim, music_unfavsim, nomusic_favsim, nomusic_unfavsim) 

datasim <- data.frame(participantsim, musicsim, species_typesim, ratingsim) 

datasim$age <- as.factor(rep(demographics_age, each = 20)) 

datasim$education <- as.factor(rep(demographics_levelofstudy, each = 20)) 

datasim$rural_urban <- as.factor(rep(demographics_area, each = 20)) 

datasim$gender <- as.factor(rep(demographics_gender, each = 20)) 

datasim$musicsim <- as.factor(datasim$musicsim) 

datasim$species_typesim <- as.factor(datasim$species_typesim) 

datasim$participantsim <- as.factor(datasim$participantsim)

modelsim_demo <- lmer( ratingsim ~ musicsim * species_typesim + age + education + rural_urban + gender + (1 | participantsim), data = datasim ) summary(modelsim_demo)

  
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# -------------------------------
# Participant-level summary for repeated measures
# -------------------------------
summary_data <- datasim %>%
  group_by(participant, musicsim, species_typesim) %>%
  summarise(mean_participant = mean(ratingsim), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(musicsim, species_typesim) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_rating = mean(mean_participant),
    se_rating = sd(mean_participant) / sqrt(n()),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# -------------------------------
# Bar graph
# -------------------------------
ggplot(summary_data, aes(x = species_typesim, y = mean_rating, fill = musicsim)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.8)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_rating - se_rating, ymax = mean_rating + se_rating),
                width = 0.2, position = position_dodge(width = 0.8)) +
  labs(
    x = "Species Type",
    y = "'I would want to care for or protect this species'",
    fill = "Music Condition",
    title = "Effect of Music and Species Type on Pro-Conservation Attitudes"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("#6693F5", "#FF9500"),
    labels = c("No Music", "Music")
  ) +
  theme_light(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12),     
    axis.text = element_text(size = 12),        
    legend.title = element_text(size = 12),     
    legend.text = element_text(size = 12),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold"))
```

```{r}
# Load packages
library(lme4)
library(dplyr)

set.seed(123)

# Number of participants
n_participants <- 40

# Simulate demographics once for all participants
demographics <- data.frame(
  participant = 1:n_participants,
  age = sample(c('16-19','20-24','25-29','30-34','35-39','40-44','45-49','50+','I choose not to say'), 
               n_participants, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.30,0.30,0.20,0.07,0.05,0.03,0.02,0.02,0.01)),
  education = sample(c('DID NOT COMPLETE HIGHSCHOOL','COMPLETED HIGHSCHOOL','CURRENTLY IN UNDERGRAD','COMPLETED UNDERGRAD','CURRENTLY IN MASTERS','COMPLETED MASTERS','IN PHD','COMPLETED PHD','I choose not to say'),
                     n_participants, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.01,0.04,0.70,0.10,0.05,0.03,0.03,0.03,0.01)),
  rural_urban = sample(c('RURAL','URBAN','I choose not to say'), n_participants, replace = TRUE),
  gender = sample(c('woman','man','non-binary person','I choose not to say'), n_participants, replace = TRUE)
)

# Function to simulate Likert ratings (0–6)
simulate_likert <- function(n, mean, sd) {
  round(pmin(pmax(rnorm(n, mean, sd), 0), 6))
}

# Initialize empty data frame
datasim <- data.frame()

# Loop over participants to simulate ratings
for (p in 1:n_participants) {
  
  # 5 ratings per condition
  music_fav <- simulate_likert(5, mean = 5.7, sd = 0.2)
  music_unfav <- simulate_likert(5, mean = 4, sd = 0.2)
  nomusic_fav <- simulate_likert(5, mean = 4.6, sd = 0.2)
  nomusic_unfav <- simulate_likert(5, mean = 1, sd = 0.3)
  
  # Combine ratings
  ratings <- c(music_fav, music_unfav, nomusic_fav, nomusic_unfav)
  musics <- c(rep("yes", 10), rep("no", 10))
  species <- c(rep("favoured",5), rep("unfavoured",5), rep("favoured",5), rep("unfavoured",5))
  
  # Combine with participant demographics
  temp <- data.frame(
    participantsim = p,
    musicsim = musics,
    species_typesim = species,
    ratingsim = ratings,
    age = rep(demographics$age[p], 20),
    education = rep(demographics$education[p], 20),
    rural_urban = rep(demographics$rural_urban[p], 20),
    gender = rep(demographics$gender[p], 20)
  )
  
  # Append to main dataset
  datasim <- rbind(datasim, temp)
}

# Convert to factors
datasim <- datasim %>%
  mutate(
    participantsim = as.factor(participantsim),
    musicsim = as.factor(musicsim),
    species_typesim = as.factor(species_typesim),
    age = as.factor(age),
    education = as.factor(education),
    rural_urban = as.factor(rural_urban),
    gender = as.factor(gender)
  )

# Fit linear mixed-effects model
modelsim_demo <- lmer(
  ratingsim ~ musicsim * species_typesim + age + education + rural_urban + gender + (1 | participantsim),
  data = datasim
)

# View summary
summary(modelsim_demo)

# Optional: Check average ratings per condition
datasim %>%
  group_by(musicsim, species_typesim) %>%
  summarise(mean_rating = mean(ratingsim),
            sd_rating = sd(ratingsim),
            .groups = 'drop')

ggplot(summary_data, aes(x = species_typesim, y = mean_rating, fill = musicsim)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.8)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_rating - se_rating, ymax = mean_rating + se_rating),
                width = 0.2, position = position_dodge(width = 0.8)) +
  labs(
    x = "Species Type",
    y = "Pro-Conservation Attitudes",
    fill = "Music Condition",
    title = "Effect of Music and Species Type on Pro-Conservation Attitudes"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("#6693F5", "#FF9500"),
    labels = c("No Music", "Music")
  ) +
  theme_light(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12),     
    axis.text = element_text(size = 12),        
    legend.title = element_text(size = 12),     
    legend.text = element_text(size = 12),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold"))
```
